fix(finops-plugin,git-plugin): replace gh repo view with git remote in context commands (#913)

* fix(finops-plugin,git-plugin): replace gh repo view with git remote in context commands

Replace `gh repo view` calls in skill context sections with `git remote`
alternatives to avoid TLS failures in some environments.

Closes #901

Co-authored-by: Lauri Gates <laurigates@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(finops-plugin,git-plugin): fix pipe and redirection errors in context commands

Replace `git remote -v | head -1` and `git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | sed ...`
patterns with `git remote get-url origin` to eliminate pipe and redirection operator
violations in skill context commands. Also remove complex owner-type detection command
that used multiple prohibited operators (pipes, redirections, ||).

Context commands run in a restricted execution engine that blocks pipe operators,
redirection operators, and shell operators.

Co-authored-by: Lauri Gates <laurigates@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use SHGetIDListFromObject to get pidlFolder instead of re-parsing the path

Re-parsing the parent directory via SHParseDisplayName can produce a
PIDL that differs subtly from what psfRaw actually represents (different
case, junction/symlink resolution, etc.). DEFCONTEXTMENU.pidlFolder +
pidlChild are combined by the Properties handler to resolve the file path;
a mismatch causes "The properties for this item are not available".

Fix: call SHGetIDListFromObject(psfRaw) to obtain the folder's own
canonical PIDL — guaranteed to be consistent with the IShellFolder we
already have, so Properties and other verbs can resolve the item path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Bootstrap eval: B=30, 5reg+5cls, crash-safe output with metadata

- Change defaults: n_bootstrap=50→30, n_reg=10→5, n_cls=10→5
- Add --n-reg / --n-cls CLI arguments for flexible configuration
- Add metadata sidecar JSON (bootstrap_meta_*.json) alongside each
  final CSV, recording all params, methods, timing, hostname
- Never overwrite: existing results with different params are archived
  to {output_dir}/_prev/ with timestamped directories
- Checkpoint stores config (B, n_reg, n_cls, seed): on resume, stale
  checkpoints from a different config are detected and archived
- Orphaned partial CSVs (from power-loss before first checkpoint) are
  detected and archived to prevent header/column mismatch corruption
- finalize_output validates rows: truncated rows from mid-append power
  loss are discarded (empty boot_id/method check)
- Cleanup of intermediate files happens ONLY after both final CSV and
  metadata sidecar are safely on disk — no gap for power-loss to leave
  an unrecoverable state
- Remove SVR/SVC from multi_model_evaluator (unused, slow)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Vedana — agent's sukham/duhkham health pulse (BG 13.6-7)

Sukham (pleasure) and Duhkham (pain) are listed in Gita 13.6-7 as
part of the Ksetra (material field). They CAN and MUST be programmed.

Vedana is the single health pulse of the agent itself (not the codebase —
that's HealthSense/GHRANA). It reads from all internal sources:
  - Provider health (alive/total, 35% weight — most critical)
  - Error pressure (recent error rate, 25% weight)
  - Context pressure (context window usage, 15% weight)
  - Synaptic confidence (Hebbian weight average, 15% weight)
  - Tool success rate (execution outcomes, 10% weight)

Produces VedanaSignal with 0.0-1.0 health + guna derivation:
  > 0.7 = sukham/sattva, 0.3-0.7 = rajas, < 0.3 = duhkham/tamas

Wired into StewardAgent:
  - agent.vedana property reads live health
  - get_state() includes vedana breakdown

Prerequisite for Cetana (heartbeat adapts to health) and BuddyBubble
(peers read each other's vedana).

13 behavioral tests (zero dataclass inspection).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use SHCreateDefaultContextMenu with explicit registry keys to load all extension handlers

Replace IShellFolder::GetUIObjectOf with SHCreateDefaultContextMenu —
the same function Explorer uses internally. The key difference is that
DEFCONTEXTMENU.aKeys lets us pass the registry keys (HKCR\*, HKCR\<ext>,
HKCR\<ProgID>, HKCR\AllFilesystemObjects) that the shell uses to enumerate
dynamic context menu handlers. Without these, only static verbs from the
IShellFolder itself appear (e.g. "Run as administrator" from HKCR\exefile\
shell\runas), while COM-based extension handlers like 7-Zip and Notepad++
(registered under HKCR\*\shellex\ContextMenuHandlers) are skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: auto-publish Docker image to GHCR (#283)

* ci: add Docker image auto-publish to GHCR

Adds GitHub Actions workflow to build and push Docker images to
GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io/zylos-ai/zylos-core).

Triggers:
- Push tag v* → publishes :v0.3.6, :0.3, :latest
- Push to main (Docker-related files only) → publishes :latest

Features:
- Multi-platform: linux/amd64 + linux/arm64 (covers NAS/ARM users)
- Build cache via GitHub Actions cache (faster rebuilds)
- Uses GITHUB_TOKEN (no extra secrets needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: use :main tag for dev builds, :latest only on release tags

- Push to main → :main (testing/dev)
- Push tag v* → :1.2.3, :1.2, :latest (stable release)

Follows the pattern used by Immich, Open WebUI, and Dify.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: install zylos-core from local source instead of GitHub URL

The Dockerfile was doing `npm install -g https://github.com/zylos-ai/zylos-core`
which always installs main HEAD, regardless of which tag/commit is being
built. Changed to COPY the local source and install from it, so the image
always matches the exact commit/tag being built.

This aligns with how install.sh resolves the latest release tag, and is
the standard pattern for Dockerfile builds in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove build directive from docker-compose.yml

The compose file is meant for end users who curl-download it and run
`docker compose up -d`. It should only reference the pre-built GHCR
image, not include build configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: zylos-01 <zylos-01@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix WQP cron: expand lookback to 12 months and add missing characteristics

State agencies upload to WQP with 6-12 month lag — the 6-month window
only captured 19 records for MD. With 12 months: 7,323 records.

Added characteristics that state agencies actually use:
- Total Nitrogen mixed forms (NH3/NH4/NO2/NO3), Nitrate + Nitrite
- Total Phosphorus mixed forms, Orthophosphate
- Enterococcus (5,112 MD records alone), Fecal Coliform
- Conductivity, TSS, Chlorophyll a

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: support pre-uninstall lifecycle hook (#284)

Run pre-uninstall hooks declared in SKILL.md before stopping services
and removing files during component uninstall. Hook failure warns but
does not block the uninstall.

Environment variables passed to the hook:
- ZYLOS_COMPONENT: component name
- ZYLOS_SKILL_DIR: skill directory path
- ZYLOS_DATA_DIR: data directory path

Closes #251

commit.message:
Fix MGRS NaN: toPoint returns 2-element array, not 4 v0.47.2

The mgrs library's toPoint() returns [lon, lat] (2 elements) for
exact points, not [minLon, minLat, maxLon, maxLat] (4 elements).
Accessing indices 2/3 gave undefined, averaging with undefined
produced NaN for all coordinates.

commit.message:
refactor: split provider monolith + fix silent failures + cull noise tests

Provider split (1021 → 3 files):
- provider/chamber.py: ProviderChamber routing/failover (500 lines)
- provider/adapters.py: Google/Mistral/Anthropic translation (300 lines)
- provider/__init__.py: build_chamber + re-exports (zero import breakage)

Silent failure fixes (engine.py):
- JSON parse: _extract_json_object() brace-matching fallback recovers
  tool calls from preamble text and malformed markdown fences
- JSON-like content that fails parse now logs WARNING (was silent)
- Provider exhaustion: error event includes provider stats (was generic)
- Token tracking: logs when usage reports 0/0 (CBR was flying blind)

Test quality:
- Culled 15 shallow tests (dataclass defaults, mock structure assertions)
- Added 7 deep JSON extraction tests (preamble, malformed fences, nested)
- 850 tests, higher signal-to-noise ratio

Dead code: removed _resolve_dynamic_context() from agent.py (superseded
by 5 Jnanendriyas — GitSense/ProjectSense cover what it was supposed to do)

commit.message:
fix: pass hwndHost to GetUIObjectOf, add SetForegroundWindow + OleInitialize

Three fixes:

1. Create helper window BEFORE GetUIObjectOf and pass it as hwndOwner.
   Some shell extensions (7-Zip etc.) inspect hwndOwner during context
   menu creation and silently skip their items when it is null or from
   a foreign process.

2. Call SetForegroundWindow(hwndHost) before TrackPopupMenuEx. Without
   this the menu's internal message loop never receives keyboard events,
   so Escape (and all other keys) are ignored.

3. Call OleInitialize/OleUninitialize on the STA thread. Some shell
   handlers require OLE to be fully initialised (not just CoInitialize)
   before they register their menu items. PostMessage(WM_NULL) after
   TrackPopupMenuEx flushes leftover messages per MSDN guidance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
